name: Chicken and Cashew Nuts Sichuan Style
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1. Combine 1 tablespoon soybean paste, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 2 teaspoons sugar, 1/2 tablespoon vinegar, 1/4 teaspoon salt in a bowl.
2. Dice the chicken breasts into half-inch pieces. Dip each piece first in egg white, then in cornstarch mixed with a pinch of salt.
3. Heat oil in a pan until hot and fry the diced chicken. Drain excess oil.
4. Slice green peppers vertically, remove seeds, stems, and pith. Cut into half-inch squares.
5. Cut parboiled bamboo shoots to match the size of the peppers.
6. In a wok, heat 2 tablespoons oil and stir-fry garlic, bamboo shoots, and peppers until fragrant.
7. Add chicken pieces and cashew nuts. Stir in rice wine.
8. Pour in the sauce mixture and briefly stir fry over high heat. Add MSG (optional) to enhance flavor.
